title: Job Openings at MMT Consults
content:
MMT Consults is a dynamic Human Resource Consulting and General Services firm dedicated to delivering innovative, practical, and sustainable solutions that empower organizations to achieve excellence. We partner with businesses across diverse sectors to optimize their people, processes, and performance, ensuring growth, efficiency, and long-term success.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Kitchen Supervisor

Job Location: Lifecamp, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Supervise and coordinate the activities of kitchen staff
	Ensure food is prepared and presented according to standards
	Monitor food quality, taste, and portion control
	Maintain cleanliness and hygiene in line with health and safety regulations
	Oversee food preparation processes and kitchen workflow
	Manage inventory, stock levels, and ordering of supplies
	Minimize food wastage and control kitchen costs
	Train and guide kitchen staff on proper procedures
	Ensure proper use and maintenance of kitchen equipment
	Handle and resolve basic kitchen-related issues during service


Requirements


	Minimum of OND or relevant culinary certification
	Proven experience in a kitchen or food service environment
	Basic knowledge of food safety and hygiene standards
	Strong leadership and team management skills
	Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced environment
	Good communication and organizational skills
	Attention to detail and commitment to quality.


Salary
N70,000 - N100,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 10th May, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

 

2.) Book Keeper

Job Location: Lifecamp, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Record daily sales, expenses, and payments accurately
	Maintain up-to-date financial records and ledgers
	Reconcile cash, POS, and bank transactions
	Track inventory costs (food, drinks, and supplies)
	Monitor and record supplier invoices and payments
	Prepare basic financial reports (daily, weekly, monthly)
	Assist in payroll processing and expense tracking
	Ensure proper filing of receipts and financial documents
	Support budgeting and cost control efforts.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND/HND in Accounting, Finance, or related fields
	Basic knowledge of bookkeeping and accounting principles
	Experience with accounting software or spreadsheets (e.g., Excel)
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Good organizational and time management skills
	Integrity and ability to handle confidential information.


Application Closing Date: 13th May, 2026. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.



3.) Sales Representative

Job Location: Wuse 2, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Greet and attend to customers in a friendly and professional manner.
	Assist customers in selecting fabrics based on their needs and preferences.
	Provide information on fabric types, prices, and uses.
	Process sales transactions (cash, POS, or transfers).
	Arrange and display fabrics neatly in the store.
	Restock shelves and monitor inventory levels.
	Maintain cleanliness and organization of the store.
	Handle customer inquiries and resolve basic complaints.
	Posting on social media platfofrms.


Salary
N90,000 - N100,000 monthly.

Application Closing Date: 10th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Vacancies at Lotus Capital Limited
content:
Lotus Capital Limited is a full-service ethical investment management company specializing in Asset Management, Private Wealth Management, and Financial Advisory services. Lotus Capital is a pioneer in non-interest finance in Nigeria and duly registered with the Securities & Exchange Commission (SEC) as Fund Managers.

Over the last 14 years, we have consistently contributed towards the growth of non-interest finance in the Nigerian capital market, and also manage 3 mutual funds listed on the Nigerian Stock Exchange.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Risk Analyst
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos
Department: Enterprise Risk Management
Reports To: Head of Risk

Job Purpose


	To support the identification, measurement, monitoring, and reporting of credit, market, liquidity, and operational risks across all investment portfolios and business activities, ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements and internal risk appetite.


Key Responsibilities
Risk Identification & Assessment:


	Monitor risks across all portfolios (fixed income, equities, alternatives, and funds).
	Support risk and control self-assessments (RCSA) exercise across departments.


Market & Investment Risk Monitoring:


	Monitor mark-to-market (MTM) movements across portfolios.
	Review portfolio concentration limits and diversification metrics.
	Monitor stop-loss thresholds and breaches.


Liquidity Risk Management:


	Prepare and monitor Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) and liquidity buffers.
	Track cash flow projections and redemption pressures through KRIs.
	Identify potential liquidity shortfalls and escalate where necessary
	Ensure adequate High Quality Liquid Assets (HQLA) are maintained.


Operational Risk Management:


	Track operational risk incidents and loss events.
	Monitor Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) across departments.
	Ensure adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
	Support business continuity and disaster recovery planning.


Compliance & Regulatory Reporting:


	Support monitoring of regulatory filings and disclosures
	Assist in audit reviews (internal and external).
	Maintain documentation for regulatory inspections.


Risk Reporting:


	Support the preparation of risk dashboards and reports for management and risk committees.
	Highlight breaches, trends, and emerging risks.


Reporting Responsibilities by Frequency Daily Responsibilities:


	Perform the Value-at-Risk computation across all funds.
	Monitor portfolio performance (MTM, P/L, yield movements)
	Track limit breaches (stop-loss, concentration, exposure limits)
	Escalate material risk issues immediately Weekly Responsibilities:
	Prepare weekly risk summary report Monthly Responsibilities:
	Assist in preparation of comprehensive risk management report for management.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or related field with 1- 2 years of xperience in Finance such as Asset Management and Banking.
	Professional certifications would be an added advantage.


Technical Skills


	Knowledge of Fixed income instruments (bonds, Sukuk, CPs, etc.) and equities.
	Familiarity with economic indices (interest rates, foreign exchange rates, inflation, etc.).


Behavioral Competencies:


	Analytical and detail-oriented
	Strong communication and reporting skills
	Excellent organizational skills
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines


Application Closing Date: 20th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Junior Research Analyst

Job Location: Lagos

Responsibilities
Market & Economic Research:


	Monitor macroeconomic trends (inflation, interest rates, FX, GDP)
	Track developments in financial markets (equities, fixed income, commodities)
	Prepare periodic market updates and economic outlook reports


Company & Industry Analysis:


	Conduct fundamental analysis of listed companies (financial statements, ratios, valuation)
	Build and maintain financial models
	Analyze industry trends and competitive positioning


Investment Support:


	Support portfolio managers with data, research, and analysis
	Monitor portfolio performance and risk metrics


Data Management & Reporting:


	Collect, clean, and analyze financial and market data
	Maintain research databases and trade logs
	Prepare reports, presentations, and dashboards for internal use


Compliance & Documentation:


	Ensure all research and trade documentation meets internal policies and regulatory standards
	Maintain proper audit trails for all investment decisions
	Support compliance reviews and internal audits


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Economics, or a related field;
	Progress toward CFA (Level I) is an advantage
	Basic understanding of portfolio management and financial markets.


Job Attributes:


	Strong analytical and quantitative skills
	Basic financial modeling and valuation knowledge
	High attention to detail
	Strong Excel and data management skills
	Understanding of financial instruments and markets
	Good written communication
	Strong sense of accountability and organization.


Application Closing Date: 20th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Portfolio Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Responsibilities


	Monitor the economy and financial markets and provide guidance for investment decisions.
	Appraise investment opportunities and prepare high quality investment recommendations
	Construct and manage investment portfolios across asset classes in line with mandates
	Perform financial and trading analysis and build valuation models
	Oversee seamless transaction execution and settlement
	Ensure accurate posting of portfolio and transaction data
	Achieve portfolio outperformance of selected benchmarks
	Conduct periodic portfolio appraisals and analysis to aid portfolio optimization
	Prepare and deliver quality strategy and performance reports
	Other related investment management duties.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Economics, or a related field; a Master’s Degree is preferred
	Professional certification in finance and/or accounting from a recognized professional body is highly valued.


Job Attributes:


	Minimum of three years of experience in a similar role.
	Experience in managing equities and fixed income is preferred.
	Strong understanding of the macroeconomic environment and capital markets
	Good understanding of trading strategies
	Strong data analysis and financial modeling skills
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills
	Strong presentation skills
	High attention to detail
	Strong ethics and integrity
	Familiarity with non-interest finance principles – added advantage.


Application Closing Date: 15th May, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Positon as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Med-In Pharmaceutical Limited - We are an indigenous company. We manufacture and sale different sizes of intravenous infusions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant
Job Location:&nbsp;Sagamu Interchange, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary&nbsp;


	The accountant is expected to manage daily financial transactions, monitors inventory, analyzes production costs, and ensures compliance with regulatory standards.
	This role involves managing the high volume of raw materials (chemicals, plastics, fluids) and finished goods associated with IV bag or pharmaceutical production.


Key Responsibilities


	Cost Accounting &amp; Inventory Management: Tracking costs of materials, labor, and overhead to determine the cost of goods sold (COGS). This involves auditing inventory accuracy, including raw materials, work-in-progress, and finished infusion products.
	Production Analysis: Analyzing production efficiency and reporting on gross margins.
	Financial Reporting &amp; Closing:Carrying outmonth-end and year-end closings, preparing profit and loss statements, and reconciling bank statements.
	Regulatory &amp; Quality Compliance: Ensuring all financial procedures adhere to company policies, generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP), and potentially QMS (Quality Management System) or ISO standards related to manufacturing.
	Accounts Payable/Receivable: Processing vendor invoices (for raw materials) and managing customer invoicing.
	Budgeting &amp; Forecasting:&nbsp;Development of operational budgets and financial forecasts.


Required Skills &amp; Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	Experience: Previous experience in a manufacturing or production environment is strongly preferred
	Technical Skills: Proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Tally) and advanced Excel skills are critical.
	Analytical Skills: Strong ability to analyze complex data sets regarding inventory, costs, and margins.
	Knowledge of import permit processing


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to:&nbsp;alexanderejere@medinpharm.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Accountant Job at Med-In Pharmaceutical Limited




        
    

        
        Med-In Pharmaceutical Limited - We are an indigenous company. We manufacture and sale different sizes of intravenous infusions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accountant
Job Location: Sagamu Interchange, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The accountant is expected to manage daily financial transactions, monitors inventory, analyzes production costs, and ensures compliance with regulatory standards.
	This role involves managing the high volume of raw materials (chemicals, plastics, fluids) and finished goods associated with IV bag or pharmaceutical production.


Key Responsibilities


	Cost Accounting & Inventory Management: Tracking costs of materials, labor, and overhead to determine the cost of goods sold (COGS). This involves auditing inventory accuracy, including raw materials, work-in-progress, and finished infusion products.
	Production Analysis: Analyzing production efficiency and reporting on gross margins.
	Financial Reporting & Closing:Carrying outmonth-end and year-end closings, preparing profit and loss statements, and reconciling bank statements.
	Regulatory & Quality Compliance: Ensuring all financial procedures adhere to company policies, generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP), and potentially QMS (Quality Management System) or ISO standards related to manufacturing.
	Accounts Payable/Receivable: Processing vendor invoices (for raw materials) and managing customer invoicing.
	Budgeting & Forecasting: Development of operational budgets and financial forecasts.


Required Skills & Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	Experience: Previous experience in a manufacturing or production environment is strongly preferred
	Technical Skills: Proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., SAP, Oracle, Tally) and advanced Excel skills are critical.
	Analytical Skills: Strong ability to analyze complex data sets regarding inventory, costs, and margins.
	Knowledge of import permit processing


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Assurance (QA) Officer &ndash; Renewable Energy&nbsp;

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Vet and approve installers before onboarding onto the Instollar platform
	Review installer submissions, credentials, and supporting documentation
	Conduct quality checks on installation projects using reports, images, and feedback
	Identify quality issues, risks, and non-compliance in projects
	Escalate critical issues to relevant internal teams
	Maintain records of QA reviews, findings, and decisions
	Ensure consistency in quality standards across all installers and projects


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Electrical/Electronic Engineering
	Proven experience in renewable energy systems (solar, inverter, or hybrid installations)
	Experience in quality assurance, inspection, operations, or technical review roles
	Ability to assess technical work using documentation and visual evidence
	Strong attention to detail and sound judgment
	Ability to make decisions with incomplete or real-world information
	Good communication and reporting skills.

Quality Assurance (QA) Officer – Renewable Energy Job at Instollar LLC




        
    

        
        Instollar LLC - Number 1 green energy talent marketplace that connects renewable energy companies in need of skills for project implementation across Africa with freelance green-collar talents with experience in off-grid solar energy projects.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Assurance (QA) Officer – Renewable Energy 

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Vet and approve installers before onboarding onto the Instollar platform
	Review installer submissions, credentials, and supporting documentation
	Conduct quality checks on installation projects using reports, images, and feedback
	Identify quality issues, risks, and non-compliance in projects
	Escalate critical issues to relevant internal teams
	Maintain records of QA reviews, findings, and decisions
	Ensure consistency in quality standards across all installers and projects


Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Electrical/Electronic Engineering
	Proven experience in renewable energy systems (solar, inverter, or hybrid installations)
	Experience in quality assurance, inspection, operations, or technical review roles
	Ability to assess technical work using documentation and visual evidence
	Strong attention to detail and sound judgment
	Ability to make decisions with incomplete or real-world information
	Good communication and reporting skills.


     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Bookkeeper",
  "description": "At JP Gramm, we bring people together through hospitality, comfort, and unforgettable experiences.&nbsp;Our brand combines a boutique hotel, a vibrant restaurant, a lively lounge, and a versatile event space; all designed to serve Lagos and beyond.&nbsp;We are more than just a hospitality business. We are a lifestyle hub where guests can relax, dine, celebrate, and connect. Whether it&rsquo;s a corporate meeting, a family event, a night out with friends, or a weekend getaway, JP Gramm is built to make every moment memorable.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bookkeeper

Job Location:&nbsp;Idimu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (On-site)
Department: Finance &amp; Accounts
Reports To: Finance Manager
Business Units: Hotel &middot; Lounge &middot; Restaurant &middot; Event Centre

About the Role


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and dependable entry-level Bookkeeper to support the finance team across our integrated hospitality operations.
	This role covers four business units &mdash; a hotel, lounge, restaurant, and event centre and offers an excellent foundation for a career in hospitality finance, with hands-on exposure to multi-revenue-stream accounting.


Key Responsibilities
Daily Accounting:


	Record and reconcile daily sales figures from the hotel, lounge, restaurant, and event centre in the accounting system
	Reconcile POS terminal reports and bank settlements on a daily basis
	Post journal entries accurately and maintain the general ledger
	Process petty cash disbursements and manage replenishment requests


Accounts Payable &amp; Receivable:


	Track and file supplier invoices across food &amp; beverage, housekeeping, and events procurement
	Follow up on outstanding guest invoices and manage corporate billing records
	Assist with event deposit processing, payment collection, and post-event reconciliations
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date vendor payment schedules


Reporting &amp; Compliance:


	Prepare weekly expense summaries for each of the four business units
	Support month-end close activities, account reconciliations, and trial balance preparation
	Assist in the preparation of VAT and WHT schedules as directed by the Finance Manager
	Maintain organised digital and physical filing of all financial documents and vouchers


Inventory &amp; Cost Support:


	Assist with periodic stock counts for the restaurant, bar, and housekeeping stores
	Record inventory receipts and cross-reference against purchase orders
	Flag discrepancies in food &amp; beverage cost reports promptly to the Finance Manager
	Support event costing exercises and contribute to post-event profitability reviews


Requirements
Required:


	OND, HND, or BSc in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
	Basic understanding of double-entry bookkeeping principles
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel (formulas, pivot tables)
	Strong numerical accuracy and attention to detail
	Good written and verbal communication skills in English


Preferred / Advantageous:


	0 &ndash; 2 years of bookkeeping or accounts experience (NYSC counts)
	Familiarity with accounting software (Sage, QuickBooks, or similar)
	Prior exposure to a hospitality or retail environment
	Student membership of ICAN or ACCA is an added advantage


Key Skills &amp; Attributes:


	High level of integrity and discretion when handling confidential financial data
	Ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously under deadline pressure across concurrent business units
	Collaborative team player capable of working effectively with non-finance departments such as front desk, kitchen, and events
	Eagerness to learn, take initiative, and grow within the hospitality finance function
	Flexibility to work on weekends or public holidays during peak occupancy periods or scheduled events


What We Offer


	Salary: N150,000 - N200,000 monthly&nbsp;commensurate with qualifications and experience
	Structured mentorship and guidance from senior finance personnel
	Broad exposure to a diverse, fast-paced multi-unit hospitality environment


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a brief cover letter to:&nbsp;chiamaka@jpgramm.com&nbsp;using &quot;Entry-Level Bookkeeper Application&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note:&nbsp;Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Bookkeeper Job at JP Gramm




        
    

        
        At JP Gramm, we bring people together through hospitality, comfort, and unforgettable experiences. Our brand combines a boutique hotel, a vibrant restaurant, a lively lounge, and a versatile event space; all designed to serve Lagos and beyond. We are more than just a hospitality business. We are a lifestyle hub where guests can relax, dine, celebrate, and connect. Whether it’s a corporate meeting, a family event, a night out with friends, or a weekend getaway, JP Gramm is built to make every moment memorable.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bookkeeper
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Idimu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (On-site)
Department: Finance & Accounts
Reports To: Finance Manager
Business Units: Hotel · Lounge · Restaurant · Event Centre

About the Role


	We are seeking a detail-oriented and dependable entry-level Bookkeeper to support the finance team across our integrated hospitality operations.
	This role covers four business units — a hotel, lounge, restaurant, and event centre and offers an excellent foundation for a career in hospitality finance, with hands-on exposure to multi-revenue-stream accounting.


Key Responsibilities
Daily Accounting:


	Record and reconcile daily sales figures from the hotel, lounge, restaurant, and event centre in the accounting system
	Reconcile POS terminal reports and bank settlements on a daily basis
	Post journal entries accurately and maintain the general ledger
	Process petty cash disbursements and manage replenishment requests


Accounts Payable & Receivable:


	Track and file supplier invoices across food & beverage, housekeeping, and events procurement
	Follow up on outstanding guest invoices and manage corporate billing records
	Assist with event deposit processing, payment collection, and post-event reconciliations
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date vendor payment schedules


Reporting & Compliance:


	Prepare weekly expense summaries for each of the four business units
	Support month-end close activities, account reconciliations, and trial balance preparation
	Assist in the preparation of VAT and WHT schedules as directed by the Finance Manager
	Maintain organised digital and physical filing of all financial documents and vouchers


Inventory & Cost Support:


	Assist with periodic stock counts for the restaurant, bar, and housekeeping stores
	Record inventory receipts and cross-reference against purchase orders
	Flag discrepancies in food & beverage cost reports promptly to the Finance Manager
	Support event costing exercises and contribute to post-event profitability reviews


Requirements
Required:


	OND, HND, or BSc in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
	Basic understanding of double-entry bookkeeping principles
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel (formulas, pivot tables)
	Strong numerical accuracy and attention to detail
	Good written and verbal communication skills in English


Preferred / Advantageous:


	0 – 2 years of bookkeeping or accounts experience (NYSC counts)
	Familiarity with accounting software (Sage, QuickBooks, or similar)
	Prior exposure to a hospitality or retail environment
	Student membership of ICAN or ACCA is an added advantage


Key Skills & Attributes:


	High level of integrity and discretion when handling confidential financial data
	Ability to manage multiple tasks simultaneously under deadline pressure across concurrent business units
	Collaborative team player capable of working effectively with non-finance departments such as front desk, kitchen, and events
	Eagerness to learn, take initiative, and grow within the hospitality finance function
	Flexibility to work on weekends or public holidays during peak occupancy periods or scheduled events


What We Offer


	Salary: N150,000 - N200,000 monthly commensurate with qualifications and experience
	Structured mentorship and guidance from senior finance personnel
	Broad exposure to a diverse, fast-paced multi-unit hospitality environment


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a brief cover letter to: [email protected] using "Entry-Level Bookkeeper Application" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply
